hooks.command: CMD=$(cat | jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); if printf '%s' "$CMD" | grep -qE '(^|[;&|] *)bun test( |$)' && ! printf '%s' "$CMD" | grep -qE 'bun run test'; then echo 'BLOCKED: Use `bun run test` not `bun test` — bun test uses the built-in runner which ignores vitest config.' >&2; exit 2; fi

CMD=$(cat | jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); if printf '%s' "$CMD" | grep -qiE 'deploy.*mainnet|upgrade.*mainnet|vercel.*--prod'; then echo 'PRODUCTION DEPLOYMENT — Requires explicit user confirmation before proceeding'; fi
CMD=$(cat | jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); if printf '%s' "$CMD" | grep -qE 'git.*push.*--force.*(main|master)'; then echo 'BLOCKED: Never force push to main/master' >&2; exit 2; fi
CMD=$(cat | jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); if printf '%s' "$CMD" | grep -qE '(^|[;&|] *)git commit'; then cd "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" && bun lint --quiet 2>&1 | tail -5; fi; exit 0

CMD=$(cat | jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); if printf '%s' "$CMD" | grep -qE '(cat|head|tail|less|more|source|bat)\s+(\./)?\.(env|env\.[a-z]+)' || printf '%s' "$CMD" | grep -qE 'grep\s+.*\s+(\./)?\.(env|env\.[a-z]+)'; then echo 'BLOCKED: Direct .env file access via Bash is not allowed. Secrets must not be read in Claude sessions.' >&2; exit 2; fi

FILE=$(cat | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty'); if [ -n "$FILE" ] && echo "$FILE" | grep -qE 'packages/contracts/src/'; then printf '%s\n' 'CRITICAL SURFACE: packages/contracts/src/**' '  Read every touched line before you finish this change.' '  Run the contracts-security reviewer flow on this diff before merge.' '  Verify access control, storage layout, external calls, and invariant preservation.' '  Log-only failure handling is not acceptable here; add an explicit remediation path instead.'; fi; exit 0

FILE=$(cat | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty'); if [ -n "$FILE" ] && echo "$FILE" | grep -qE 'packages/shared/src/providers/(Auth|JobQueue|Work)\.tsx|packages/shared/src/modules/job-queue/|packages/shared/src/hooks/(auth|work|vault|blockchain)/'; then printf '%s\n' 'CRITICAL SURFACE: shared auth, queue, or mutation path.' '  Read every touched line before you finish this change.' '  Run the mutation-reliability reviewer flow when async or mutation behavior changes.' '  Verify failure states are explicit to the caller or UI and that retries or replays stay coherent.' '  Log-only catch blocks are not acceptable here; add a concrete recovery or user-visible error path instead.'; fi; exit 0

FILE=$(cat | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty'); if [ -n "$FILE" ] && echo "$FILE" | grep -qE '\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx|json)$' && ! echo "$FILE" | grep -q 'packages/contracts/out/'; then npx @biomejs/biome format --write "$FILE" 2>/dev/null; fi; exit 0

echo '## Post-Compaction Reminders
- Use `bun run test` (NEVER `bun test`) — bun test ignores vitest config
- Never use `forge build`/`forge test` directly — always use bun wrappers
- ALL hooks MUST live in packages/shared/src/hooks/ (not client or admin)
- Single .env at root only — never create package-specific .env files
- Import from `@green-goods/shared` barrel only — never deep paths
- Use `Address` type (not string) for Ethereum addresses
- Use `logger` from shared (not console.log)
- Use `parseContractError()` + `USER_FRIENDLY_ERRORS` for contract errors
- Critical surfaces require line-by-line review; log-only failure handling is never enough on auth, queue, or contract paths'
